title: grevillea coccinea subsp. lanata
intro: Grevillea coccinea subsp. lanata, also known as woolly red grevillea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. Traditionally, Grevillea coccinea subsp. lanata has been used by Aboriginal people for its medicinal properties, including treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and can be prepared into teas, infusions, and salves to harness its health benefits.
